title: notopleura polyphlebia
intro: Notopleura polyphlebia, also known as "Quina", is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimalarial, and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its alkaloid content. The health benefits of Notopleua polyphlebia include the treatment of fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to prevent and treat malaria. Notopleura polyphlebia is commonly prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts for medicinal use, although its use should be under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to its potential side effects and interactions.
